address: Flat 2, 63 Albert Road, South Norwood SE25 4JD
lead_profile_text: Bridge STL strategy — looking to sell 1-bed flat but lacking confidence in near-term sale price. Already self-listing on STL platform, struggling with weekday occupancy. Plans to handle all furnishing herself; no setup quote needed.
emotional_profile: Pragmatic and cautious. Already in the STL world but underperforming. Wants proof that weekday occupancy is solvable. Practical focus on cost clarity and essential setup requirements.
green_light_trigger_phrases: I'm getting lots of weekend stays but not getting weekday stays
I really want to get more contractors

top_objections.text: Weekday occupancy — not getting enough midweek bookings currently
top_objections.zac_response: NHS/healthcare and contractor demand in SE25; direct booking strategy targets midweek corporate guests

top_objections.text: Not confident about achieving asking price on sale
top_objections.zac_response: STL as bridge strategy — income while property remains on market, exit flexibility with 3-month notice

top_objections.text: Wanted clarity on essential equipment requirements before listing
top_objections.zac_response: Itemised essential vs non-essential list provided: smart thermostat, key safe, fire blanket/detectors essential; cameras not required

top_objections.text: Whether income projections are achievable given current underperformance
top_objections.zac_response: Low competition in SE25, direct booking potential, Selhurst Park demand driver — projections justified by market data

top_questions.answer: Not essential — Airbnb prohibits interior cameras; exterior optional but not required

top_questions.text: Do I need a smart thermostat?
top_questions.answer: Yes — essential for remote management; low cost, high value for guest satisfaction

top_questions.text: What type of guests will the property attract?
top_questions.answer: NHS/healthcare workers primary weekday guest type for SE25; Selhurst Park (Crystal Palace FC) events drive weekend demand

what_moved_them: Low competition in SE25 — less competitive for bookings
Direct booking potential for the area was positive data point
Property amenities noted as strong vs local competition
Cameras not required — removed perceived barrier/cost
Smart thermostat framed as essential remote management tool at low cost
what_fell_flat: Income projection credibility uncertain given current self-managed underperformance

conversion_gaps.gap: Weekday occupancy problem not demonstrably solved in meeting
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Send SE25 weekday occupancy data or NHS/contractor booking case study before June 22 expiry

conversion_gaps.gap: No next meeting to follow up offer expiry
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Proactive outreach before June 22 — email or call to confirm decision

offer_framing: 13% + VAT vs standard 15% — framed as limited window to lock in reduced rate before June 22
zac_income_intro: Low competition in SE25, direct booking potential high — property amenities strong vs comparable listings in the area
zac_transition_phrases: The NHS and contractor demand in SE25 is exactly what fills midweek gaps
The 3-month rolling notice means you can exit cleanly when a buyer comes through
zac_reassurance_phrases: Smart thermostat is low cost and gives you full remote control
You don't need cameras — Airbnb prohibits interior ones anyway
